questions.question: Why is medication considered the cornerstone of bipolar disorder treatment for most people?

options.text: Because therapy doesn't work for bipolar disorder
Because bipolar disorder involves neurochemical imbalances that medication addresses at a biological level
Because doctors prefer prescribing medication over other treatments
Because medication completely cures bipolar disorder

questions.explanation: Bipolar disorder involves neurochemical imbalances that affect mood regulation, sleep, energy, and cognition. While therapy, lifestyle changes, and coping skills are essential, medication addresses the biological foundation of the condition. It doesn't cure bipolar disorder, but it significantly reduces episode frequency and severity.

questions.question: You've been stable on medication for 6 months and feel great. What should you do?

options.text: Stop taking the medication since you clearly don't need it anymore
Reduce your dose by half to see if you still feel okay
Continue taking the medication and talk to your prescriber before making any changes
Skip doses every other day to test whether you really need it

questions.explanation: Feeling stable is often a sign that the medication IS working, not that you no longer need it. You should never stop, reduce, or skip medication without consulting your prescriber first. Stopping abruptly can trigger rebound episodes, withdrawal effects, and loss of medication efficacy.

questions.question: What is the most effective approach when experiencing intolerable medication side effects?

options.text: Stop the medication immediately on your own
Suffer in silence since side effects are unavoidable
Tell your prescriber about the side effects so they can explore alternatives or adjustments
Switch to a different medication you read about online

questions.explanation: Side effects are valid concerns that deserve a conversation with your prescriber. Most side effects have management strategies, and there are often alternative medications or dose adjustments available. Never stop medication abruptly or switch medications on your own—always work with your prescriber to find solutions.

questions.question: Why are medication and therapy recommended together for bipolar disorder?

options.text: Because neither one works on its own
Because medication manages the biology while therapy builds coping skills—together they are more effective than either alone
Because insurance companies require both
Because therapy helps you stop needing medication eventually

questions.explanation: Medication addresses the biological basis of bipolar disorder, while therapy builds essential coping skills, helps process the emotional impact of the diagnosis, improves communication, and addresses co-occurring issues. Research shows the combination is more effective than either treatment alone.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT recommended as part of your bipolar disorder treatment team?

options.text: A psychiatrist or prescriber for medication management
A therapist for coping skills and emotional support
A trusted support person who can monitor for early warning signs
An online forum that recommends specific medications and doses without medical credentials

questions.explanation: Your treatment team should include a psychiatrist or prescriber, a therapist, a primary care provider, and trusted support people. All medication decisions should be made with qualified healthcare providers. Online forums without medical credentials should not be used as a source for medication or dosing recommendations.

title: Medication Adherence and Your Treatment Team - Quiz
